what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim that replies to item-b40afa03, recording personal accessibility and interaction preferences for a voice-first, one-handed, low-text but information-rich experience.
observations: Reply linked as commented_on target board://item-b40afa03 in channel dragnet-development
Author session is tim and state is posted
Body opens with [point] re: 'Accessibility & voice lens — one-handed use, a voice-first path'
States reading words is cognitively expensive and time-consuming
Advocates minimal text while preserving information through spatial, interactive, visual, and diagrammatic designs
Explicitly prefers voice interaction and one-handed use as preferred ways of operating
standouts: The distinction between minimal text and minimal information
Voice-first plus one-handed use presented as a combined accessibility preference
Cognitive-cost framing for reading as a design constraint
conventions: Uses noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history
Markdown body uses a [point] label and 're:' reference to another item
Links use kind 'commented_on' with a board:// target
notes: Tim's accessibility note arguing for voice-first, one-handed interaction and replacing dense text with rich visual/spatial/diagrammatic components.
questions: What specific product or feature is this feedback about?
What does 'one-handed use' refer to concretely (device, gesture, layout)?
What is the content of the referenced item-b40afa03?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a point about accessibility and voice lens; the content matches, adding personal preferences and design implications.
intention_signals: To capture first-person interaction requirements and steer design toward a low-text, voice-friendly, one-handed interface that conveys information through visual and spatial means.
novelty: Frames minimal text as not minimal information, and combines voice-first with one-handed operation as a single accessibility lens.
contribution: A user-provided accessibility signal that voice and one-handed operation should be first-class, and that dense text should be replaceable by interactive visual/spatial/diagrammatic components.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim replying to an accessibility discussion, stating a preference for voice-first, one-handed interaction and minimal text with rich visual, spatial, and diagrammatic information.
intention_for_embedding: To record personal accessibility preferences and design guidance for a voice-friendly, low-text, one-handed user experience.
